technologies.name: Pinecone
technologies.description: Fully managed vector database purpose-built for similarity search. Offers serverless and pod-based architectures.

technologies.name: pgvector
technologies.description: PostgreSQL extension for vector similarity search. Supports HNSW and IVFFlat indexing. Ideal for teams already using PostgreSQL.

technologies.name: Weaviate
technologies.description: Open-source vector database with built-in vectorization modules, hybrid search, and multi-tenancy support.

technologies.name: Qdrant
technologies.description: High-performance vector similarity search engine with filtering, payload storage, and Rust-based performance.

technologies.name: Milvus
technologies.description: Cloud-native vector database built for scalable similarity search. Supports billion-scale vectors with GPU acceleration.

technologies.name: Chroma
technologies.description: Developer-focused embedding database. Simple API, great for prototyping and small-scale RAG applications.

technologies.name: Snowflake
technologies.description: Cloud data platform with separated compute and storage, supporting structured and semi-structured data at scale.

technologies.name: Databricks
technologies.description: Unified analytics and AI platform built on Apache Spark. Combines data lake and data warehouse via Delta Lake.

technologies.name: BigQuery
technologies.description: Google's serverless, multi-cloud data warehouse. Supports ML directly in SQL via BigQuery ML.

technologies.name: Apache Kafka
technologies.description: Distributed event streaming platform for high-throughput, low-latency data pipelines and streaming applications.

technologies.name: Apache Flink
technologies.description: Stream and batch processing framework with exactly-once semantics, event time processing, and stateful computations.

technologies.name: Apache Spark
technologies.description: Unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing. Supports SQL, streaming, ML, and graph processing.

technologies.name: Apache Airflow
technologies.description: Workflow orchestration platform for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring data pipelines.

technologies.name: Dagster
technologies.description: Data orchestrator with software-defined assets, built-in testing, and observability. Modern alternative to Airflow.

technologies.name: dbt
technologies.description: SQL-based transformation tool that enables analytics engineers to transform data in the warehouse using software engineering practices.

technologies.name: MLflow
technologies.description: Open-source platform for ML lifecycle management including experiment tracking, model registry, and deployment.

technologies.name: Weights & Biases
technologies.description: ML experiment tracking, dataset versioning, and model management platform with rich visualization.

technologies.name: Feast
technologies.description: Open-source feature store for ML serving and training. Provides consistent feature access across offline and online stores.

technologies.name: Amazon SageMaker
technologies.description: AWS managed service for building, training, and deploying ML models. Includes notebooks, training jobs, and endpoints.

technologies.name: Vertex AI
technologies.description: Google Cloud's unified ML platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI models including generative AI.

technologies.name: vLLM
technologies.description: High-throughput LLM serving engine with PagedAttention for efficient memory management. Optimized for production inference.

technologies.name: Claude
technologies.description: Anthropic's frontier AI model family. Known for safety, long context, and strong reasoning. Available via API and cloud platforms.

technologies.name: GPT-4
technologies.description: OpenAI's multimodal large language model. Widely adopted for text generation, analysis, and code. Available via API and Azure.

technologies.name: Llama
technologies.description: Meta's open-weight LLM family. Strong performance at various sizes, enabling on-premise and custom deployment.

technologies.name: Gemini
technologies.description: Google's multimodal AI model family with native multi-modal understanding and long context windows.

technologies.name: Mistral
technologies.description: European AI lab's efficient open-weight models. Known for strong performance-to-size ratio and mixture-of-experts architecture.

technologies.name: LangChain
technologies.description: Framework for building applications powered by LLMs. Provides chains, agents, memory, and retrieval components.

technologies.name: LlamaIndex
technologies.description: Data framework for LLM applications focused on ingestion, indexing, and querying of private data. Strong RAG capabilities.

technologies.name: Amazon Bedrock
technologies.description: AWS managed service providing access to foundation models from multiple providers. Supports fine-tuning and RAG.

technologies.name: Collibra
technologies.description: Enterprise data governance and catalog platform. Provides data quality, lineage, and compliance management.

technologies.name: DataHub
technologies.description: Open-source metadata platform for data discovery, observability, and governance. Extensible via plugins.

technologies.name: Great Expectations
technologies.description: Data quality testing framework. Define expectations for data and validate them in pipelines.

technologies.name: Amazon Web Services
technologies.description: Leading cloud platform with the broadest service portfolio. Dominant in enterprise cloud infrastructure.

technologies.name: Google Cloud Platform
technologies.description: Google's cloud platform, strong in data analytics, AI/ML, and Kubernetes. BigQuery and Vertex AI are differentiators.

technologies.name: Microsoft Azure
technologies.description: Microsoft's cloud platform with strong enterprise integration. Azure OpenAI Service is a key AI differentiator.

capabilities.name: Vector Similarity Search
capabilities.description: Search for semantically similar items using vector embeddings.

capabilities.name: Real-time Feature Serving
capabilities.description: Serve ML features with low latency for online inference.

capabilities.name: Experiment Tracking
capabilities.description: Track ML experiments including parameters, metrics, and artifacts.

capabilities.name: Stream Processing
capabilities.description: Process continuous data streams in real time.

capabilities.name: Batch Processing
capabilities.description: Process large volumes of data in scheduled batches.

capabilities.name: Data Warehousing
capabilities.description: Store and analyze structured data at scale with SQL interfaces.

capabilities.name: Workflow Orchestration
capabilities.description: Schedule, coordinate, and monitor complex data pipelines.

capabilities.name: Text Generation
capabilities.description: Generate human-like text for various tasks including writing, analysis, and conversation.

capabilities.name: Code Generation
capabilities.description: Generate, explain, and debug code across programming languages.

capabilities.name: LLM Serving
capabilities.description: Serve large language models with high throughput and low latency.

capabilities.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
capabilities.description: Combine retrieval from a knowledge base with LLM generation for grounded responses.

capabilities.name: Data Quality Validation
capabilities.description: Validate data against defined expectations and quality rules.

capabilities.name: Metadata Management
capabilities.description: Catalog, discover, and govern metadata across the data estate.

capabilities.name: Model Training
capabilities.description: Train ML models at scale with managed compute resources.

patterns.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
patterns.description: Retrieve relevant context from a knowledge base and feed it to an LLM to generate grounded, accurate responses.
when_not_to_use: Data is already in the model's training set
Real-time latency is critical and retrieval adds too much overhead

patterns.name: Agentic AI
patterns.description: LLM-powered agents that can plan, use tools, and take actions autonomously to accomplish complex tasks.

patterns.name: Feature Store
patterns.description: Centralized repository for storing, managing, and serving ML features consistently across training and inference.
when_to_use: Need consistent features between training and serving

patterns.name: Lakehouse Architecture
patterns.description: Combines data lake flexibility with data warehouse performance. ACID transactions on object storage with SQL query capabilities.
when_to_use: Need both structured analytics and unstructured data
Want to avoid maintaining separate lake and warehouse
when_not_to_use: Simple analytics on structured data only
Small data volumes where a warehouse suffices

patterns.name: Data Mesh
patterns.description: Decentralized data architecture where domain teams own and serve their data as products.
when_to_use: Centralized data team is a bottleneck

decision_templates.question: Which vector database should we use for our similarity search / RAG needs?
What should be our core data platform / warehouse?
Which workflow orchestrator should we use for our data pipelines?
Which foundation model should we use for our AI applications?
Which experiment tracking tool should we use for our ML workflows?
